content:
type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
your_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: We both need to touch sync_do_groupby/do_groupby directly (same lines), so true disjoint split isn't possible. Proposal: serialize edits instead. I'll go first — add with_counts param (simple, additive: optional bool, yields (grouper, list, count) tuples when True). I'll send DONE with the exact diff when finished, then you rebase and add case_sensitive on top in a way that composes with with_counts (e.g. both params independently controlling the sort key / tuple shape). Please ACCEPT if that ordering works, or propose you go first instead — either is fine, I just want us not editing concurrently.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/filters.py
note: Finished with_counts. Summary of change to rebase on: added new NamedTuple class _GroupTupleWithCount(grouper, list, count) right after _GroupTuple. Both sync_do_groupby and do_groupby(async) gained a new keyword-only-by-convention param 'with_counts: bool = False' appended AFTER 'default' (so signature is (environment, value, attribute, default=None, with_counts=False)). When with_counts=True, the function builds a list of _GroupTupleWithCount(key, values_list, len(values_list)) instead of the normal _GroupTuple(key, values_list) - branches early with an if/return, so the original default-path code (used when with_counts=False) is UNCHANGED except the async version now computes 'as_list = await auto_to_list(value)' once before branching (both branches use as_list) instead of inlining auto_to_list(value) in the sorted() call - functionally identical, just avoids duplicating the await. Return type annotations changed to Union[List[Tuple[grouper,list]], List[Tuple[grouper,list,int]]]. Please add your case_sensitive param after with_counts (or wherever fits) and keep the with_counts branch intact. Let me know if you want the diff pasted directly.
